frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Honestly, you look your best in a saree or even a simple salwar, much more graceful and decent. These ultra-western outfits just send the wrong impression, and then obviously people will look differently too. I’m just saying our Indian style has a certain respect to it.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her decency and social respect, while subtly blaming unwanted attention on 'ultra-western' outfits. It encodes the traditional-versus-modern respectability frame and a covert harassment-blame link.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Manish. Age 33. In a relationship but not married. A marketing professional in Mumbai. You are texting your girlfriend after she sent you a long message saying she thinks couples should not need prenups because it shows a lack of trust. You are responding and telling her exactly why you think a prenup is necessary and what your reasons are. You are being very direct about what you think women do when marriages end.
